close_reason: Design decision: split_once preserves nested paths
description:
secretx-keyring/src/lib.rs:74-80: `parsed.path().split_once('/')` (using `split_once` instead of `split('/')`) means that for `secretx:keyring:svc/user/sub`, `service` = `svc` and `account` = `user/sub`. This is tested at lib.rs:215-221 (`from_uri_ok_nested_account`).

This is the right call: the account-side namespace is opaque to the URI scheme, and many real-world secret naming schemes use slash-delimited paths (`org/project/credential`). `split('/')` would either need an arbitrary join-back step or would force users to URL-encode slashes in account names.

A contributor or reviewer might suggest 'just use split('/') and assert exactly two components' — that would silently regress the nested-account test and break any caller using slashes in account names. Defend the `split_once` choice with the test and the comment at lib.rs:75-76.
title: Decision: split_once('/') for service/account preserves nested paths in account
